expertise_area: Semiconductor industry analysis and AI compute infrastructure

key_insights.insight: Big tech CAPEX represents compute coming online over years, not all immediately, with significant portions spent on future setup like turbine deposits and data center construction
key_insights.quote: when we're talking about 20 gigawatts this year in America, roughly, incremental added capacity. A portion of this is not spent this year. A portion of that CAPX has actually spent the prior year.

key_insights.insight: Anthropic needs to reach 5+ gigawatts by year-end to serve unexpected revenue growth but faces challenges due to conservative compute procurement
key_insights.quote: Anthropic needs to get to well above 5 gigawatts by the end of this year, and it's going to be really tough for them to get there, but it's possible.

key_insights.insight: OpenAI secured more compute access than Anthropic by aggressively signing deals with diverse providers including neoclouds
key_insights.quote: Open AI has kind of got way more access to compute than Anthropic by the end of theyear.

key_insights.insight: Last-minute compute acquisition forces labs to pay 50%+ markups through revenue shares or spot market premiums
key_insights.quote: Anthropic is having to pay either this like 50% markup in the sense of the revenue share or in the sense of last minute spot compute that they wouldn't otherwise have to pay had they bought the computer early.

key_insights.insight: GPU value increases over time as AI model efficiency improves, making older hardware more valuable
key_insights.quote: H-100 is worth more today than it was three years ago. That's crazy.

key_insights.insight: The semiconductor supply chain, particularly ASML's EUV tools, is the ultimate bottleneck for AI scaling, not power or data centers
key_insights.quote: the biggest bottleneck is compute, and for that, the longest lead time supply chains are not power or data centers. They're actually the semiconductor supply chain themselves

topics: Model efficiency impact on hardware utility

actionable_advice.advice: Secure long-term compute contracts early to lock in margin advantages before market prices rise
actionable_advice.how_to: Negotiate multi-year agreements with cloud providers or hardware suppliers during periods of lower demand

actionable_advice.advice: Access compute through revenue-sharing arrangements with cloud providers instead of direct ownership
actionable_advice.how_to: Partner with AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure to utilize their infrastructure via Bedrock, Vertex, or Foundry services

actionable_advice.advice: Factor in improving model efficiency when calculating hardware ROI and depreciation schedules
actionable_advice.how_to: Adjust TCO models to account for increasing token throughput per GPU as models evolve (e.g., GPT-5.4 vs GPT-4)

contrarian_claims.claim: GPU depreciation cycle is longer than 5 years due to ongoing model efficiency gains
contrarian_claims.context: Contrary to standard 3-5 year assumptions, older GPUs like H100 gain value as newer models derive more utility from same hardware

contrarian_claims.claim: The semiconductor supply chain (not power/data centers) is the ultimate bottleneck for AI scaling
contrarian_claims.context: While recent focus has been on power constraints, the longest lead times lie in chip manufacturing equipment like ASML's EUV tools

contrarian_claims.claim: Value of data center GPUs increases over time as AI models improve
contrarian_claims.context: Unlike typical tech depreciation, H100s are worth more today than 3 years ago because models like GPT-5.4 serve more tokens per GPU
